lines.text: Looks like they're slowly finding their way.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: No need to rush—I, too, am curious to see what truths await them.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: The grand revelry draws closer and closer...
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Ah, we've arrived. Such a small world, isn't it, {PlayerName}?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Oh, what an honor to be remembered! Let's continue our conversation somewhere lively and grand, a place worthy of the Carnevale, not some dreary, dull corner.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: They say the thrill of the chase surpasses the joy of the catch. And with this view of Ragunna, one can truly savor its splendor.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Oh, don't fret. I've been invited to this grand feast as a conductor.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Decadence, indulgence… Hehe, that's the Carnevale—a vivid daydream and grand illusion for all.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: No need to worry, I won't disrupt their little daydream. Besides, we still have business to do here.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Now that you know what makes the Sentinel so unique, you should know we seek the same thing. That extraordinary Resonator.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: How about I share a little story with you? A gesture of goodwill for our cooperation.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Now, now. Patience is a virtue. A story is best enjoyed with a captivating performance, and this stage is perfectly set for it.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: It's fine. I do appreciate the noble lady's sentiment—a wise investment often bears fruit in the most unexpected ways.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: We're simply spreading the truth through a tale. I'm certain it will pique your interest, so consider this a heartfelt invitation to you and your companions.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Join us, pour your essence into this endless play, and use your humble strength to defy the raging current of fate!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: And so we arrive at the "happily ever after," yes?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: But we do not live in a fairy tale. There is no playwright to script a happy ending for us... What lies behind the curtain is but a cruel jest, a dark satire.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Let me reveal the truth behind that idyllic ending. The Holy Maiden has long succumbed to darkness, drawing power from a heretical force and leading her people into depravity.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: And the minister, having uncovered this truth, has vowed to root out this corruption himself.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Ah, come now. Let us embrace the grand climax of this revelry!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Why, there's no rule against an actor making good use of the intermission.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Besides, I haven't disrupted your arrangement, have I?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: "The one from a faraway land who discerns the truth dares to raise their sword against the divine. In response, the slumbering gods break free from their shackles, revealing their true forms..."
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: The possibility of the Second Resonance Awakening is now a certainty. So we just need to see if that special Resonator truly exists.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Was the Blessed Maiden a Resonator for the Sentinel or the Threnodian?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Or both? If so... Oh, a Resonator capable of wielding both powers... I am eager to see how this story ends.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Cristoforo, if you don't want to spoil the next act, I might have to give your carefully crafted plot a little nudge.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Speaking of which, {PlayerName} shines brighter than anyone else in your play. Do you find {Male=him;Female=her} interesting as well?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: …Perhaps.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: From Huanglong to Rinascita, {Male=he;Female=she} has indeed added a few unexpectedly beautiful notes to my symphony.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Hmm... I see. Doesn't matter. That’s not important for our goal.
We have the answers we wanted already.
—With the Second Resonance Awakening, we will tap into powers that rival the Sentinels... Complete control of the artificial Resonator Awakening process is within reach.
And lastly, about that Rover...
This trip was worth it. We've collected many wonderful notes, haven't we?
It's about time.
Get moving. Can't let a certain someone wait too long behind bars.
Hmm... I see. Doesn't matter. That’s not important for our goal.
We have the answers we wanted already.
—With the Second Resonance Awakening, we will tap into powers that rival the Sentinels... Complete control of the artificial Resonator Awakening process is within reach.
And lastly, about that Rover...
This trip was worth it. We've collected many wonderful notes, haven't we?
It's about time.
Get moving. Can't let a certain someone wait too long behind bars.
This is but an illusion. One of the night, one of dreams. Not like the spectacle you saw in the glimmer of the Carnevale.
Here, I am not your foe, nor can we harm each other, should we so wish. Lay your blade to rest, if only for now. Tis' a pleasure to see you again, {PlayerName}, on this special stage.
A city wild and beautiful, chasing endless glory... Tell me, does Septimont please you as much as Ragunna once did?
Ah, so it is she you seek. It appears this special Resonator has you rather captivated.
When one wrestles against the torrents of fate, every signpost is an anchor guiding you toward the infinite horizon.
Let's take our time. I would like our talk to be pleasant. No one will bother us here.
I have an associate who adores the heroic tales told throughout this land.
For this reason...  the missing girl you seek and the anomaly you sensed in Septimont, they are indeed threads in his tapestry.
You could call it... an obsession of his.
Every hero's tale begins with a trial.
Your wisdom and courage, your compassion and resolve, your hope and madness... Will they be strong enough to carry you through the trial? To make you the hero of this story?
Read it. I hope this story speaks to you. The answers you seek lie within these pages.
If we continue to meet as foes, the symphony grows monotonous. I confess I find it... rather uninspired.
I'm giving this story a little nudge.
As of now, you and that Blessed Maiden from the tower are deeply interwoven in the fate he has spun.
It's not as you imagine. You've already met my associate, you see. He trusts what he sees, not the words of others.
As a creator, he always hopes his characters will craft twists and turns that he himself cannot foresee.
He believes that the closer one stands to the protagonist, the greater their influence over the tale.
But the true lead of this story has yet to emerge.
I gave you this <te href=850153>play</te> so you can see the ending. That Gladiator girl will be the harbinger of the Dark Tide in Septimont.
All glory will be consumed, and the pride of heroes will dissolve within a blackened maelstrom. Such is the finale "she" holds most dear.
This is a short story, {PlayerName}. One that only lasts three days. When the final match concludes, so too will this story.
You could claim the spotlight by ending her life before the curtain falls. Change the ending, and the tragedy is averted.
But should you falter, or worse, warn her of what's to come, the consequences will spiral far beyond anything you can imagine.
You don't need to trust me. Let my words slip from your thoughts, if you wish. As I've already said, the story will unfold as my associate has written it.
The choice lies with you, {PlayerName}. It is a freedom we all hold.
Even the wildest imaginations draw from what already exists.
So it is with this story. It follows a template, and even its creator cannot escape its confines.
Choose wisely, {PlayerName}. Time is still on your side, for now.
I look forward to your performance in this grand finale.
It is where the Order of the Deep crafts Common Echoes.
Yes. The Orchestration Altar is there.
However...
Keep your eyes sharp. The mist is another trap they've set for us.
The left one.
Still in doubt?
Suit yourself.
It seems the chaos has seeped into every corner of the Fabricatorium…
Yes. The Orchestration Altar is behind that pillar.
Water? It's the energy source that keeps this site running.
Hold on. I'll orchestrate this area first. Let's see if the path will reveal itself after the mist is cleared.
(It's… possible...?)
This mist… It's even thicker than before.
And...
A sharp observation.
Worry not. It won't affect our goal.
There is always a way forward. Stay close.
Does it matter?
A daughter just lost her father. There's no need for a father to lose his daughter too.
You didn't even strike.
Do you know what a musician does if she plays the wrong note?
She plays on. No matter how broken, how off-key, the melody never stops.
You didn't even ask what I could offer.
Heh.
Then let's make a deal. I'll seal your father within the shrine, where he shall wait for a true warrior to grant him a final, glorious death.
As for you, I'll use the Lumenstones as anchors of my Orchestration to reshape your body. With a few notes of dissonance, of course.
Yes. You will be a piece of music composed by the Dark Tide.
This pact will hold until the last Lumenstone goes dark. I wonder how long you'll endure the Dark Tide's corruption. A day? Or a century?
We'll see, Gladiator. We'll see.
Oh? I didn't think it would be this easy.
You're here to watch the duel, aren't you, Medulius?
Seems you're dimmer than most villains, and twice as reckless as your average halfwit.
But since you've been so generous offering yourself to me as a vessel, I'll grant you a wish in return.
I'll let you live long enough to see your little duel. So long that you'll be begging for death before the end.
You lost the Gem I entrusted to you, Cristoforo.
Tell me, how much of <ano=Leviathan>It</ano> remains within you?
...I've fulfilled my promise to complete your finale. Let this be the last note of our symphony.
If this world truly cannot change, perhaps I will seek a new path for myself.
Everything is made of frequencies. Once the melody is distorted, its rhythm is skewed. No blunt force can ever rectify it.
Unless one orchestrates its essence anew.
After you.
The Gem… is fleeing into the Dark Tide?
Says the one who hasn't witnessed the years, the failures, the countless attempts I've made. What gives you the audacity to pass such judgment?
Among thousands of paths, surely one will lead me back to them.
And even if I'm wrong... I fear not starting over. I've done it time and time again.
I know they're there.
"The villagers are dead..."
So, you saw them in their human form.
Then how can you claim my goal is unattainable?!
The Fractsidus showed me a way forward.
Even if they have their own motives, even if we're only using each other... I care not.
If they can help me take this step, then another step may follow. And another. No matter how steep the price, I'll pay it, step by step, until I reach the end I seek.
Love? Sincerity? Compassion? Trust?
What you and your cronies hold dear is but a jest to the Fractsidus. No bond lasts forever. Without true loyalties, there can be no betrayal, no deceit.
I no longer buy into such illusions.
After all, even if you believe with all your heart, even if you wait patiently at a crossroads...
The one you're waiting for may have long since forgotten you. Forgotten the promise they swore to keep.
I waited. For a long time.
I won't wait anymore.
What was once an unforgettable encounter between us can now be the thread that weaves a paradise for "them."
Heh, you're one to talk. How amusing.
You, with your long life and its endless possibilities, still choosing to start over, even at the cost of all your memories?
The irreplaceable bonds, the emotions that make us human, are the brightest lights and most precious treasures in our fragile existence.
What madness, what cruelty, what impossible goal would drive you to cast them all aside?
You've gone further than I ever could.
Perhaps you believe more than I do that only in endless time can true, eternal perfection arise. That only then will a brave new world arrive.
We walk different paths, yet in the end, we are both driven by the same madness. Why argue who's fallen further?
The only difference is that... you've forgotten it all.
Nor have you stepped beyond the boundary that death has placed upon the human soul.
Hehe...
So, this is our finale.
I'm sorry.
I've come home.
The Gem's power grows.
What I couldn't do back then… I can make happen now...
Your bones will no longer be marrowless. Your blood will no longer be cold...
Yes, it will...
The world is but a symphony of resonating frequencies. Humans may fall to be Tacet Discords, and Tacet Discords may rise again as humans... There is no divide between us.
I have long erased that line. So can everyone else.
